name: Google Workspace
tagline: Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, and Sheets from one credential.
description: Google Workspace exposes its services through per-product REST APIs built on the Google Discovery Service, a vendor-published CLI (`gws`), and a popular community CLI (`gog`). All of them authenticate through Google Cloud OAuth — the setup is the hard part, and it's shared across every interface.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Desktop client)
methods.note: Bring your own Google Cloud project; both CLIs and the APIs use it.

methods.label: Service account
methods.note: For headless and Workspace-admin scenarios via domain-wide delegation.

auth.guide:
Everything Workspace-related authenticates through a Google Cloud project — set one up once and every interface below can use it.

**Vendor CLI (`gws`, easiest):** if you have `gcloud` installed, one command bootstraps the project, enables APIs, and logs you in:

```bash
brew install googleworkspace-cli
gws auth setup   # one-time; later sessions: gws auth login
```

Credentials are encrypted at rest with the key in your OS keyring. For CI, export credentials and point `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CREDENTIALS_FILE` at the file. Gotcha: OAuth apps in testing mode are capped at ~25 scopes — log in with only the services you need (`gws auth login -s drive,gmail,sheets`).

**Community CLI (`gog`):** create a Desktop-app OAuth client in the [Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials), download the client JSON, then:

```bash
brew install openclaw/tap/gogcli
gog auth credentials ~/Downloads/client_secret.json
gog auth add you@gmail.com --services gmail,calendar,drive
```

Gotcha: OAuth apps in "External + Testing" status get refresh tokens that expire after 7 days — publish the app to make them durable.

**REST APIs directly:** the same OAuth client works against every per-product API (`gmail.googleapis.com`, `www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3`, …) with `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`. Service accounts with domain-wide delegation cover admin automation, but don't replace OAuth for consumer accounts.

interfaces.name: gws
interfaces.install: brew install googleworkspace-cli && gws auth setup
interfaces.note: Published in Google's googleworkspace org (Apache-2.0) but labeled "not an officially supported Google product". Built dynamically from the Discovery Service; ships agent skills.

interfaces.name: Workspace REST APIs
interfaces.note: Per-product APIs (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets…) described by the Google Discovery Service.

interfaces.name: gog
interfaces.install: brew install openclaw/tap/gogcli && gog auth add you@gmail.com
interfaces.note: MIT-licensed, multi-account, keyring-backed token storage. No Google affiliation.
